insight: There's been no measurable rain lately, so there's no runoff driving contamination, and strong summer sunlight is helping disinfect the water. The model's main signals right now: the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low; the lack of recent rain is keeping runoff-driven contamination low; and limited sunlight is raising risk (less UV to disinfect the water). The last lab sample (5 MPN/100mL) is now 10 days old — beaches are sampled about weekly, so a reading this stale carries little weight, and this estimate leans on current conditions. It's a naturally clean, well-flushed open-coast beach (unsafe only ~3% of the time), so outside of storms it's reliably safe.
